annotations.title: Missing seen-marker boundary
annotations.message: Only mark annotations seen after they are rendered.
annotations.rawDetails: The marker should be written from the final fix-code checks payload.

annotations.title: Missing seen-marker boundary

instructions: Decide for each item under `## Failing checks`, `## Check annotations` whether a code change is warranted. **If any code changes are needed:** cancel in-progress runs first, apply edits, commit, rebase, push. **If no code changes are needed:** no push is needed.
For each failing check under `## Failing checks`: for `external` entries (no run ID, has URL): open the URL to inspect the failure.

For each item under `## Check annotations`: inspect the referenced file range and decide whether the annotation requires a code change. These annotations are surfaced once per PR and do not need any resolve/minimize mutation.
If you applied code edits: commit them with a descriptive message, then rebase onto `origin/main` per your repository's conventions before pushing.

For any large decisions or rejections you made this iteration, run `pr-shepherd journal 42 '- <decision>'` to append an entry to the `## Shepherd Journal` section. For threads and comments, use the markdown link shown in its heading above; for reviews, reference the review ID. The command is idempotent — re-running with the same text is a no-op.
Stop this iteration — if you pushed new commits, CI needs time before the next tick; otherwise stop before the next tick. Recheck: rerun `pr-shepherd 42` to recheck once after a fresh 30s–4m delay.
